verdicts.reason: The hallucination detection logic is robust, with strong error handling, path traversal guards, ReDoS protection, and comprehensive test coverage.
The file-resolution path-containment is correctly implemented, and other potential attack surfaces (ReDoS, untrusted input) are adequately mitigated.
The auto-downgrade mechanism with a clear prefix in the reason field improves user experience by reducing false-positive blocks and providing transparent feedback.
The auto-downgrade mechanism is highly discoverable due to explicit modification of the verdict's reason field and clear documentation in the changes.
Automates the detection and correction of LLM hallucinations, preventing false-positive BUGS objections and improving council reliability.
verdicts.evidence: Addresses a known weakness of LLMs (hallucination) in a critical automated review system, demonstrated by historical fixture replays.

verdicts.reason: This feature introduces a clever, targeted mechanism to auto-correct LLM hallucinations, reducing friction and improving the perceived intelligence of the council.
The proposed changes align with the pattern of auto-downgrading recurring issues and incorporate lessons learned regarding security (ReDoS, path traversal) and robustness.
